page: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in single-core and specific multi-threaded tasks. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while the Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores place in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are highly efficient, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing also demonstrate strong performance. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32as_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for mainstream enterprise workloads without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage configuration but allowing users to pay only for the exact storage performance tier needed. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, text and image processing, and moderate database operations. It is also capable of handling small-scale LLM inference tasks, demonstrating strong prompt processing and text generation speeds with smaller models.
description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on x86_64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. While it lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong Geekbench multi-core and cryptographic capabilities, alongside average memory bandwidth and database throughput. This resource profile offers a balanced, cost-effective solution for general-purpose enterprise applications. It is highly suited for web serving, software compilation, image processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor and 128 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for enterprise workloads and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a 32-vCPU general-purpose VM with an AMD EPYC processor and 128 GB RAM, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and strong single-core performance for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB RAM.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong cryptographic execution.
Premium Storage capable configuration without bundled local storage or GPUs.
Strong performance in software compilation, image processing, and small-model LLM inference.
Optimized for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, and database operations.
